rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) bromide-mediated epoxidation of alkenes on a BiVO4 photoanode using NaBr as redox mediator in H2O/CH3CN
electrochemical_role: Photogenerated holes at the BiVO4 photoanode oxidize Br- to Br2 (mediator); Br2 disproportionates in water to give the electrophilic [Br]+ (HBrO) that initiates alkene activation. Cathode performs HER, producing OH- needed for ring closure.
electron_flow: Light excites BiVO4; holes at the photoanode oxidize Br- to Br2, which disproportionates with H2O to HBrO/[Br]+ and HBr. [Br]+ adds to the alkene C=C to give a bromonium, water opens it to bromohydrin, and OH- (from cathodic HER) closes it to the epoxide while regenerating Br-.
rate_determining_step: Photoanodic oxidation of Br- to Br2 (mediator turnover), as suggested by current-limited epoxide accumulation under light-on/off control

probe_reagents.name: proton exchange membrane (two-compartment cell)
probe_reagents.observation: Anodic reaction stalls at bromohydrin 3 because OH- from the cathode cannot reach the anolyte, confirming bromohydrin as the on-cycle intermediate prior to OH--assisted cyclization

probe_reagents.name: dark control (no illumination)
probe_reagents.observation: No epoxide formed in the dark at 0.8 V; epoxide accumulates only under light, showing PEC (photo-driven) origin

probe_reagents.name: NaBr omission control
probe_reagents.observation: Only 8% epoxide yield without NaBr and water oxidation dominates; with NaBr, water oxidation is suppressed and epoxidation is selective

probe_reagents.name: NaClO4 substitution for NaBr
probe_reagents.observation: O2 evolution observed (water oxidation) instead of epoxidation, confirming Br- as the essential mediator

probe_reagents.name: GC headspace analysis
probe_reagents.observation: H2 quantified at cathode with ~100% FE; no O2 detected in sealed H-cell with NaBr, confirming suppression of OER

notes: Mechanism is adapted from established halide-mediated EC epoxidation (refs 28, 31). NaBr plays a dual role: redox mediator for epoxidation and hole scavenger that stabilizes BiVO4 against photocorrosion (loss of V5+). Dibromide (5) and diol (6) side pathways are drawn in Scheme 1 but not observed experimentally. Non-unity FE (65%) attributed to accumulation of unreacted Br2/HBrO rather than side reactions.
